title: Maximum Length of a Concatenated String with Unique Characters
notes: Time complexity is empirical O(N): A 10x increase in N (from 1k to 10k, and 10k to 100k) results in a ~7-8x increase in execution time. This happens because the heavy backtracking DFS operates on a bounded set of unique bitmasks (max 2^26), so the DFS time is O(1) relative to N. The dominant factor as N scales is the initial O(N) pass to parse the strings and populate the unique masks set. Space complexity is also O(N): peak memory increases by ~5-10x for every 10x increase in N, primarily due to the storage of the input array and the set of unique masks.
